@@ -141,7 +141,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hi7","title":"Review misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts and integrate relevant automation","description":"## Goal Review `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/misc_coding_agent_tips_and_scripts` (ignore the moonlight tip) and fold the best practices into ACFS in a way that is: - automated where safe (installer defaults / ~/.acfs assets) - documented where automation isn’t appropriate ## Scope ideas (examples) - shell ergonomics that help agent workflows - git safety patterns (non-destructive defaults, guardrails) - tmux/terminal workflows ## Constraints - Do not add “file sprawl”; integrate into existing modules/assets when possible. - Do not introduce external-contributor posture. ## Deliverables - A short decision log of what was adopted vs rejected (and why). - Concrete changes in installer/assets/docs where appropriate. ## Acceptance criteria - At least 3 high-value tips integrated (excluding moonlight). - No breaking changes to default install flow. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:53:24.838062-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:43:50.225994-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:43:50.225994-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated 2 high-value tips: (1) Git Safety Guard Hook - Python PreToolUse hook blocking destructive git/filesystem commands, (2) PATH Conflict Detection - doctor.sh now warns about bun/npm shadowing native Claude installs. Rejected: Beads guide (already integrated), Moonlight (excluded), Claude install fix (already handled by acfs.zshrc PATH ordering).","labels":["agents","docs","installer","ux"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hii","title":"Create VERSION file and version management","description":"# Task: Create VERSION File and Version Management ## Description Set up version management for ACFS releases. ## VERSION File Root of repo: `VERSION` ``` 0.1.0 ``` ## Version Format Semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH - MAJOR: Breaking changes to installer - MINOR: New features - PATCH: Bug fixes ## Version Usage ### In installer ```bash ACFS_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL .../VERSION) ``` ### In acfs doctor ``` ACFS Doctor v0.1.0 ``` ### In website Show version in footer ### In manifest ```yaml version: 1 acfs_version: 0.1.0 ``` ## Release Process 1. Update VERSION file 2. Update CHANGELOG.md 3. Tag release: `git tag v0.1.0` 4. Push: `git push --tags` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] VERSION file created - [ ] All components read version correctly - [ ] Version shown in doctor output - [ ] Version shown on website","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:50.115466-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:10:59.74241-05:00","close_reason":"VERSION file exists with 0.1.0","labels":["infrastructure","task"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-huic","title":"Docs: remove contributing mentions from README","description":"AGENTS.md policy: remove any mention of contributing/contributors from README.md. Remove the "Contributing Ideas" section and ensure the README contains no "Contribut*" language.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:57:09.658694-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:58:17.78184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:58:17.78184-05:00","close_reason":"Removed all contributing/contributors mentions from README per AGENTS.md policy."} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hun4","title":"Implement acfs-continue command for post-upgrade installation progress","description":"After Ubuntu upgrades complete with reboots, the installer continues in the background via systemd. Users need a way to see what's happening. Implement an 'acfs-continue' or 'acfs-status' command that shows the live installation progress and allows users to attach interactively. The command should: (1) Show which phase/step is currently running, (2) Show live log output, (3) Handle the case where installation is complete, (4) Be discoverable via MOTD after upgrade completes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:22:12.415448-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:22:29.110069-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hun4","title":"Implement acfs-continue command for post-upgrade installation progress","description":"After Ubuntu upgrades complete with reboots, the installer continues in the background via systemd. Users need a way to see what's happening. Implement an 'acfs-continue' or 'acfs-status' command that shows the live installation progress and allows users to attach interactively. The command should: (1) Show which phase/step is currently running, (2) Show live log output, (3) Handle the case where installation is complete, (4) Be discoverable via MOTD after upgrade completes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:22:12.415448-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:26:27.636704-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hvg","title":"Add Tailscale to doctor checks","description":"## Task Add Tailscale status to `acfs doctor` output. ## Doctor Check Implementation In `scripts/lib/doctor.sh`, add Tailscale check: ```bash check_tailscale() { echo "Tailscale:" # Binary check if ! command -v tailscale &>/dev/null; then echo " ✗ Not installed" return 1 fi local version version=$(tailscale version | head -1) echo " ✓ Installed: $version" # Daemon check if systemctl is-active --quiet tailscaled 2>/dev/null; then echo " ✓ Daemon: running" else echo " ✗ Daemon: not running" echo " Fix: sudo systemctl start tailscaled" return 1 fi # Connection check local status status=$(tailscale status --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.BackendState // "unknown"') case "$status" in "Running") echo " ✓ Status: connected" local ip hostname ip=$(tailscale ip -4 2>/dev/null) hostname=$(tailscale status --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.Self.HostName // "unknown"') echo " ✓ Tailscale IP: $ip" echo " ✓ Hostname: $hostname" ;; "NeedsLogin") echo " ⚠ Status: needs authentication" echo " Fix: sudo tailscale up" ;; "Stopped") echo " ⚠ Status: stopped" echo " Fix: sudo tailscale up" ;; *) echo " ⚠ Status: $status" ;; esac return 0 } ``` ## Output Example ``` $ acfs doctor ... Tailscale: ✓ Installed: 1.56.1 ✓ Daemon: running ✓ Status: connected ✓ Tailscale IP: 100.64.x.y ✓ Hostname: my-vps ... ``` ## Considerations - Don't fail doctor if Tailscale not authenticated (it's optional) - But DO show clear instructions on how to authenticate - Consider adding network connectivity test (ping another tailscale node?)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:58:17.753893-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:37:07.719226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:37:07.719226-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented as part of bt5 - Tailscale check exists in doctor.sh (network.tailscale checks for installed/running/connected status)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hvg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:15.295036-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","title":"TASK: Integrate SSH key prompt into installer main flow","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp (Installer SSH Key Integration) ## Summary Call the \`prompt_ssh_key()\` function at the appropriate point in install.sh, BEFORE the user normalization phase so the key can be migrated to ubuntu user. ## Implementation Details ### Integration Point In \`install.sh\`, add the SSH key prompt after initial setup but before normalize_user: \`\`\`bash # Near the start of main installation flow, after sourcing libs # Step 0: SSH Key Setup (if running as root with no key) if [[ \$EUID -eq 0 ]]; then source_lib "user.sh" prompt_ssh_key || true # Continue even if user skips/fails fi # Step 1: User normalization (existing) # This includes migrate_ssh_keys() which copies root's key to ubuntu normalize_user \`\`\` ### Why This Location 1. **Before normalize_user**: The migrate_ssh_keys() function inside normalize_user copies keys from /root/.ssh to /home/ubuntu/.ssh. We need the key installed FIRST. 2. **After sourcing libs**: We need the logging functions and prompt_ssh_key available. 3. **Root check**: Only prompt if running as root (the password-first flow). ### Error Handling The \`|| true\` ensures the installer continues even if: - User skips the key setup - Key validation fails - Any other issue with the prompt Users can always add their key later manually. ### Testing 1. Run full installer as root without existing key → should see prompt 2. Run full installer with existing key → should skip 3. After installer, verify ubuntu user has the key: \`cat /home/ubuntu/.ssh/authorized_keys\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] SSH key prompt appears early in installation - [ ] Prompt happens BEFORE normalize_user - [ ] Key is migrated to ubuntu user after prompt - [ ] Installer continues if user skips - [ ] Installer continues if key validation fails - [ ] No changes to existing flow for users with keys ## Dependencies - TASK: Implement prompt_ssh_key() function (must exist first) ## Files Modified - install.sh (add call to prompt_ssh_key)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:27.679097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: prompt_ssh_key call present in install.sh normalize_user flow","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:53.184827-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","title":"Task: Add cross-links between wizard and lessons","description":"# Add Cross-Links Between Wizard and Lessons ## Parent Feature Wizard Integration (mna) ## Goal Create bidirectional links between wizard steps and relevant lessons to help users navigate between the two learning paths. ## Wizard → Lessons Links Add "Learn more" sections to wizard steps that connect to deeper lesson content: | Wizard Step | Related Lesson | Link Text | |-------------|----------------|-----------| | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | Lesson 2: SSH & Persistence | "Struggling with SSH? [Learn SSH fundamentals →](/learn/ssh-basics)" | | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | "Need help with agent auth? [See Agent Commands →](/learn/agent-commands)" | | Step 10: Reconnect as Ubuntu | Lesson 1: Linux Navigation | "New to Linux? [Learn Linux basics →](/learn/linux-basics)" | | Step 11: Status Check | Lesson 7: Flywheel Loop | "Want to understand the tools? [See the complete workflow →](/learn/flywheel-loop)" | ### Implementation Use the existing `SimplerGuide` / `GuideTip` patterns: ```tsx // In wizard step component Want to learn more?{" "} After setup, check out our{" "} SSH fundamentals lesson {" "} for a deeper understanding. ``` ## Lessons → Wizard Links Add contextual back-links in lessons for users who haven't completed setup: ```tsx // In lesson component (conditional based on wizard completion) {!isWizardComplete && (

Not set up yet?{" "} Complete the setup wizard first →

)} ``` ### Relevant Back-Links | Lesson | Relevant Wizard Step | |--------|---------------------| | Lesson 2: SSH Basics | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | | Lesson 0: Welcome | Step 12: Launch Onboarding | ## Shared Navigation Component Consider creating a shared component for contextual navigation: ```tsx // components/related-content.tsx interface RelatedContentProps { wizardStep?: { slug: string; title: string }; lesson?: { slug: string; title: string }; workflowSection?: string; } export function RelatedContent({ wizardStep, lesson, workflowSection }: RelatedContentProps) { return (

Related Content

    {wizardStep && (
  • 📋 Setup: {wizardStep.title}
    • )} {lesson && (
  • 📚 Lesson: {lesson.title}
    • )} {workflowSection && (
  • 🔄 Advanced: {workflowSection}
    • )}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] At least 4 wizard steps link to relevant lessons - [ ] Lessons show "Not set up?" banner for incomplete users - [ ] Links use consistent styling - [ ] Navigation is helpful, not overwhelming - [ ] Mobile-friendly ## File Changes - Update: Several `apps/web/app/wizard/*/page.tsx` files - Update: Lesson pages to include back-links - Create: `apps/web/components/related-content.tsx` (optional)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:23:43.710426-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:45:24.220746-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:45:24.220746-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented wizard↔lesson cross-links","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4rd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:11.391048-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-o85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:11.586199-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:11.798668-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
